title: Are Oils-Energy Stocks Lagging  Ecopetrol (EC) This Year?
content:
The Oils-Energy group has plenty of great stocks, but investors should always be looking for companies that are outperforming their peers. Is Ecopetrol (EC) one of those stocks right now? A quick glance at the company's year-to-date performance in comparison to the rest of the Oils-Energy sector should help us answer this question.

Ecopetrol is a member of the Oils-Energy sector. This group includes 240 individual stocks and currently holds a Zacks Sector Rank of #1. The Zacks Sector Rank includes 16 different groups and is listed in order from best to worst in terms of the average Zacks Rank of the individual companies within each of these sectors.

The Zacks Rank emphasizes earnings estimates and estimate revisions to find stocks with improving earnings outlooks. This system has a long record of success, and these stocks tend to be on track to beat the market over the next one to three months. Ecopetrol is currently sporting a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy).

Over the past 90 days, the Zacks Consensus Estimate for EC's full-year earnings has moved 46.6% higher. This means that analyst sentiment is stronger and the stock's earnings outlook is improving.

Based on the latest available data, EC has gained about 49.6% so far this year. At the same time, Oils-Energy stocks have gained an average of 32.7%. This shows that Ecopetrol is outperforming its peers so far this year.

One other Oils-Energy stock that has outperformed the sector so far this year is Enerflex (EFXT). The stock is up 35.6% year-to-date.

In Enerflex's case, the consensus EPS estimate for the current year increased 20.3% over the past three months. The stock currently has a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).

Looking more specifically, Ecopetrol belongs to the Oil and Gas - Integrated - International industry, which includes 16 individual stocks and currently sits at #17 in the Zacks Industry Rank. Stocks in this group have gained about 39.7% so far this year, so EC is performing better this group in terms of year-to-date returns.

On the other hand, Enerflex belongs to the Alternative Energy - Other industry. This 51-stock industry is currently ranked #81. The industry has moved +14% year to date.

Investors with an interest in Oils-Energy stocks should continue to track Ecopetrol and Enerflex. These stocks will be looking to continue their solid performance.

title: How The Enerflex (TSX:EFX) Story Is Shifting As Analyst Views Diverge
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

The latest Enerflex update keeps the core fair value estimate steady at about CA$37.21, while some bullish analysts are now flagging upside with individual price targets such as CA$39, up from CA$28. This gap between a largely unchanged central valuation anchor and higher individual targets reflects the current mix of optimism about upside potential and caution around execution risks. As you read on, you will see how to track these shifting targets and use the evolving analyst narrative as a reference point for your own view.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for Enerflex shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Enerflex.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

TD Securities recently lifted its Enerflex price target to CA$39 from CA$28, which sits modestly above the CA$37.21 fair value anchor and signals that at least one firm sees room for additional upside. RBC Capital also raised its target by CA$3, pointing to a cluster of upward revisions that you can treat as a reference check when comparing your own assumptions to the Street’s expectations.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Raymond James issued a downgrade earlier in 2026, which serves as a reminder that not all analysts are aligned and that there are still concerns around Enerflex’s execution and risk profile. The mix of a Raymond James downgrade against higher targets from TD Securities and RBC Capital highlights a split view, so it is worth paying close attention to how the company delivers against expectations that are embedded in these targets.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!TSX:EFX 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 3 risks for Enerflex. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

From October 1, 2025 to December 31, 2025, Enerflex repurchased 102,800 shares for C$1.54 million, representing 0.08% of its shares under its ongoing buyback program. Under the buyback announced on March 19, 2025, Enerflex has repurchased a total of 2,779,000 shares for C$30.79 million, representing 2.25% of its shares. Enerflex has a Special or Extraordinary Shareholders Meeting scheduled for May 6, 2026.

How This Changes the Fair Value For Enerflex

Fair value stays at CA$37.21, with no change in the central estimate. Revenue growth assumption remains about 1.74%, in line with the prior input. Net profit margin is essentially steady at about 8.07% on each dollar of revenue. Future P/E assumption moves from 17.65x to 17.31x. The discount rate moves slightly from 6.72% to 6.75% in the updated model.

Story Continues

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company’s business story to a financial forecast and fair value, so you can see how the numbers line up with the underlying thesis. They automatically refresh when analysts update their views or new information comes out.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Enerflex to stay up to date on:

How global natural gas demand, long term U.S. contract compression agreements, and a C$1.2b backlog are tied to recurring revenue and project visibility. The push toward higher margin After Market Services and Energy Infrastructure, along with efficiency gains that have coincided with lower SG&A and reduced net debt to EBITDA of 1.3x. Key risks such as reliance on traditional natural gas infrastructure, limited decarbonization offerings, leadership transition, margin normalization in Engineered Systems, and exposure to energy cycle swings.
